title: Basic Gates: AND, OR, NOT
prompt: Build a module that outputs the AND, OR and NOT of two 1-bit inputs a and b.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: a, b. Outputs: y_and, y_or, y_not (NOT of a).
expected_output: y_and = a & b; y_or = a | b; y_not = ~a.

title: Universal Gates: NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR
prompt: Produce NAND, NOR, XOR and XNOR of two 1-bit inputs.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: a, b. Outputs: y_nand, y_nor, y_xor, y_xnor.
expected_output: Standard truth tables for each gate.

title: 2:1 Multiplexer
prompt: Select input i0 when sel=0 and i1 when sel=1.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: i0, i1, sel. Output: y.
expected_output: y = sel ? i1 : i0.

title: 4:1 Multiplexer
prompt: Route one of four 1-bit inputs to the output based on a 2-bit select.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: i[3:0], sel[1:0]. Output: y.
expected_output: y = i[sel].

title: 2-to-4 Decoder with Enable
prompt: Activate exactly one of four outputs based on a 2-bit input when enable is high; all zero when disabled.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: a[1:0], en. Output: y[3:0] (one-hot).

title: 4-to-2 Priority Encoder
prompt: Encode the index of the highest set bit; assert valid if any input is high.
constraints: Module name: top. Input: in[3:0]. Outputs: y[1:0], valid. Bit 3 has highest priority.
expected_output: valid=0 when in=0; else y = index of MSB that is 1.

title: Half Adder
prompt: Add two 1-bit numbers producing sum and carry.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: a, b. Outputs: sum, cout.
expected_output: sum = a ^ b; cout = a & b.

title: Full Adder
prompt: Add three 1-bit values (a, b, cin) producing sum and carry-out.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: a, b, cin. Outputs: sum, cout.
expected_output: sum = a^b^cin; cout = (a&b)|(cin&(a^b)).

title: 4-bit Ripple Carry Adder
prompt: Add two 4-bit numbers with carry-in, producing a 4-bit sum and carry-out.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: a[3:0], b[3:0], cin. Outputs: sum[3:0], cout.
expected_output: {cout, sum} = a + b + cin.

title: 4-bit Magnitude Comparator
prompt: Compare two 4-bit numbers and assert gt, eq, lt.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: a[3:0], b[3:0]. Outputs: gt, eq, lt.
expected_output: gt=a>b, eq=a==b, lt=a<b (exactly one high).

title: D Latch (Level Sensitive)
prompt: Build a transparent D latch: when enable is high q follows d; when low q holds.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: en, d. Output: q. Use always @(*).
expected_output: en=1 -> q=d; en=0 -> q unchanged.

title: D Flip-Flop with Async Reset
prompt: Capture d on the rising clock edge; clear q to 0 asynchronously when rst_n is low.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, d. Output: q. Use non-blocking.
expected_output: On posedge clk, q<=d; rst_n=0 forces q=0.

title: JK Flip-Flop
prompt: Implement a JK flip-flop: 00 hold, 01 reset, 10 set, 11 toggle.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, j, k. Output: q.

title: T Flip-Flop
prompt: Toggle q on each clock edge when t=1, hold when t=0.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, t. Output: q.
expected_output: q+ = t ^ q.

title: 4-bit Shift Register (SISO)
prompt: Shift serial input in by one position each clock; serial output is the last bit.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, sin. Output: sout. 4-bit internal register, shift left.
expected_output: Data appears at sout after 4 clocks.

title: 4-bit Synchronous Up Counter
prompt: Count 0..15 and wrap, with synchronous active-high clear.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, clr. Output: cnt[3:0].
expected_output: Increments each clock; clr forces 0.

title: Mod-10 (BCD) Counter
prompt: Count 0..9 then wrap to 0, asserting tc on the count of 9.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n. Outputs: cnt[3:0], tc.
expected_output: Sequence 0,1,...,9,0,...; tc=1 when cnt==9.

title: 4-bit Ring Counter
prompt: Circulate a single 1 through a 4-bit register (one-hot), reset state 0001.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n. Output: q[3:0].

title: Sequence Detector FSM (detect 1011)
prompt: Assert detected for one cycle whenever the serial input bit stream contains the pattern 1011 (overlapping).
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, in. Output: detected. Mealy or Moore allowed.
expected_output: detected pulses high when ...1011 is seen.

title: Binary to Gray Code
prompt: Convert a 4-bit binary number to Gray code.
constraints: Module name: top. Input: bin[3:0]. Output: gray[3:0].
expected_output: gray = bin ^ (bin >> 1).

title: Gray to Binary Code
prompt: Convert a 4-bit Gray code value back to binary.
constraints: Module name: top. Input: gray[3:0]. Output: bin[3:0].
expected_output: Each binary bit is XOR of all higher Gray bits.

title: Even Parity Generator
prompt: Generate an even-parity bit for an 8-bit input.
constraints: Module name: top. Input: data[7:0]. Output: parity.
expected_output: parity = XOR of all 8 bits (1 if odd number of ones).

title: Rising Edge Detector
prompt: Output a one-cycle pulse when the input signal goes from 0 to 1.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, sig. Output: pulse.
expected_output: pulse high for one clock on each 0->1 transition of sig.

title: Clock Divider by 2
prompt: Generate an output clock at half the input frequency.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n. Output: clk_div2.
expected_output: clk_div2 toggles once per input clock period (50% duty, f/2).

title: Parameterized N-bit Mux 2:1
prompt: Implement a width-parameterized 2:1 multiplexer.
constraints: Module name: top. Param WIDTH (default 8). Inputs: a[WIDTH-1:0], b[WIDTH-1:0], sel. Output: y[WIDTH-1:0].
expected_output: y = sel ? b : a.

title: Traffic Light FSM
prompt: Cycle a light through GREEN, YELLOW, RED, advancing one state per clock.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n. Output: light[1:0] (0=GREEN,1=YELLOW,2=RED).
expected_output: GREEN -> YELLOW -> RED -> GREEN ...

title: 8-bit Barrel Shifter (Logical Left)
prompt: Logically shift an 8-bit value left by a 3-bit amount.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: din[7:0], shamt[2:0]. Output: dout[7:0].
expected_output: dout = din << shamt (zeros fill from the right).

title: Synchronous FIFO
prompt: Design a synchronous FIFO with full/empty flags using always_ff.
constraints: Module name: top. Params WIDTH, DEPTH (power of 2). Inputs: clk, rst_n, wr, rd, din. Outputs: dout, full, empty.
expected_output: Writes when !full, reads when !empty; flags track occupancy.

title: Round-Robin Arbiter (4 requesters)
prompt: Grant one of four requests in round-robin order, advancing priority after each grant.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, req[3:0]. Output: gnt[3:0] (one-hot).
expected_output: Exactly one grant; fairness rotates among active requesters.

title: One-Hot FSM: Pulse Generator
prompt: Using one-hot encoding, output a single-cycle pulse every 3rd clock.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n. Output: pulse. Use one-hot states.
expected_output: pulse high once every three clocks.

title: Assertion: Request Followed by Acknowledge
prompt: Write a concurrent assertion that every req is acknowledged by ack within 1 to 4 cycles.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, req, ack. Bind the property; disable during reset.
expected_output: Property passes when ack arrives within 4 cycles of req.

title: Assertion: One-Hot Check
prompt: Assert that a 4-bit state vector is always exactly one-hot.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, state[3:0]. Use $onehot.
expected_output: Fails if zero or more than one bit is set.

title: Constrained Random Packet Class
prompt: Create a transaction class with a random 8-bit length constrained to 1..64 and a 2-bit kind, and a method to print it.
constraints: Class name: packet. Fields: rand len[7:0], rand kind[1:0]. Constraint len inside [1:64].
expected_output: randomize() always yields 1<=len<=64.

title: UVM Sequence Body
prompt: Write the body() of a uvm_sequence that creates, randomizes and sends 10 items.
constraints: Sequence class my_seq #(my_item). Use start_item/finish_item and a loop of 10.
expected_output: Ten randomized items are sent to the driver via the sequencer.

title: APB Slave Read/Write
prompt: Implement a simple APB slave with a single 32-bit register, supporting read and write with PREADY always high.
constraints: Module name: top. APB inputs: pclk, presetn, psel, penable, pwrite, paddr, pwdata. Outputs: prdata, pready.
expected_output: Write in access phase updates reg; read returns reg; pready=1.

title: Write Basic SDC Constraints
prompt: Write the SDC commands to create a 100 MHz clock on port clk and set input/output delays of 2 ns relative to it.
constraints: Use create_clock, set_input_delay, set_output_delay. Clock period 10 ns.
expected_output: A 10 ns clock named clk with 2 ns IO delays on all inputs/outputs.
starter_code:
# Write your SDC constraints here
# clock port: clk, all inputs: [all_inputs], all outputs: [all_outputs]
solution_code:
create_clock -name clk -period 10.0 [get_ports clk]
set_input_delay  -clock clk 2.0 [all_inputs]
set_output_delay -clock clk 2.0 [all_outputs]
set_clock_uncertainty 0.2 [get_clocks clk]
